wine_festivals.name: Vinitaly
wine_festivals.recurrence_pattern: annual, first or second week of April
wine_festivals.description: The world's largest professional wine and spirits fair. Over 4,000 exhibitors from 140 countries fill the Veronafiere complex each April. Italian regions dominate the main pavilions; Veneto producers occupy Pavilion 11 and part of Pavilion 10. Saturday before the main fair hosts Operawine by Wine Spectator.

wine_festivals.name: Amarone Opera Prima
wine_festivals.recurrence_pattern: annual, late January or early February
wine_festivals.host_organization: Consorzio per la Tutela dei Vini Valpolicella
wine_festivals.description: The Consorzio Vini Valpolicella presents the new Amarone della Valpolicella vintage and select Riservas to international trade and press in Verona. Typically held at Palazzo della Gran Guardia, the two-day event is trade and press on day one, consumers on day two. Formerly known as Anteprima Amarone.

wine_festivals.name: Primavera del Prosecco Superiore
wine_festivals.recurrence_pattern: annual, first two weeks of May
wine_festivals.host_organization: Consorzio Conegliano Valdobbiadene Prosecco Superiore DOCG
wine_festivals.description: A walking and cycling trail through the UNESCO Prosecco Hills of Conegliano-Valdobbiadene. Producer cellars along the Strada del Prosecco open for free and ticketed tastings across 12 days. The 20-stage tasting passport covers Brut, Extra Dry, Dry and Rive single-vineyard expressions. One of Italy's best-organised wine-tourism walking events.

wine_festivals.name: Soave Versus
wine_festivals.host_organization: Consorzio di Tutela del Vino Soave
wine_festivals.description: The Soave Consorzio's annual comparative tasting forum held in and around the medieval castle town of Soave. The format pairs Soave Classico and DOC expressions with regional food producers. Critics and sommeliers debate Garganega's identity and the case for Soave as a serious age-worthy white wine.

wine_festivals.name: Festa dell'Uva di Bardolino
wine_festivals.recurrence_pattern: annual, late September to early October
wine_festivals.description: Bardolino's harvest festival on the lakefront promenade, celebrating the Corvina and Corvinone grapes that make both Bardolino Classico DOC and the celebrated Chiaretto di Bardolino DOC rose. Open-air pours, local fish dishes and lake views across to the Sirmione headland.

wine_festivals.name: VinNatur Villa Favorita
wine_festivals.address: Villa Favorita, Via Favari 6, 36054 Montebello Vicentino, Italy
wine_festivals.description: Europe's premier natural wine fair, held at the 17th-century Villa Favorita in Montebello Vicentino by the VinNatur association founded by Angiolino Maule of La Biancara (Gambellara). Around 200 producers from across Europe and beyond show no-additive natural wines across two days. Trade day followed by a public day.

wine_festivals.name: Cantine Aperte in Valpolicella
wine_festivals.address: Via Jago 2, 37024 Negrar di Valpolicella, Italy
wine_festivals.description: The national Italian Open Cellars day sees participating Valpolicella, Soave and Bardolino estates open free of charge for self-guided visits and tastings. The Veneto chapter is typically the largest participant in the national movement. Shuttle buses from Verona Porta Nuova serve the Valpolicella valley route.

wine_festivals.name: Amarone e Dintorni, Fumane
wine_festivals.recurrence_pattern: annual, late spring or early summer
wine_festivals.host_organization: Comune di Fumane and local producers
wine_festivals.description: A smaller, village-scale Valpolicella wine event in Fumane, one of the five sub-zones of Valpolicella Classico. Producers from the immediate Fumane valley open their cellars for free tastings and guided walks through the terraced Corvina vineyards. Amarone and Valpolicella Classico Superiore are the principal pours.
